data.title: Defense ETFs Rise amid Trump's Threats to Iran
data.description:
President Donald Trump has deployed the USS Gerald R. Ford, the world's largest aircraft carrier, to the Middle East. Although nuclear talks with Iran continue, he warned that military action remains possible. Indeed, he said that he will decide within 10 days whether to order a strike. Notably, the Ford will join about a dozen U.S. warships that are already in the region. Officials say that the move is meant to pressure Iran into a negotiated settlement while showing that force is a credible backup option.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Separately, military leaders explained that aircraft carriers provide strong strategic flexibility, as they can operate near conflict zones and launch up to 120 sorties per day. Meanwhile, Iran's Supreme Leader responded with threats, but analysts say such rhetoric is typical during tense negotiations. However, reports indicate that F-16s, F-22s, F-35s, refueling tankers, electronic warfare aircraft, and likely submarines have also been positioned closer to Iran.

Although indirect talks in Geneva have shown limited progress, major differences between the two sides remain. U.S. officials continue to stress that diplomacy is preferred, but they are preparing for multiple outcomes. For investors monitoring defense trends, diversified funds such as the Invesco Aerospace & Defense ETF (PPA), the iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF (ITA), and the SPDR S&P Aerospace & Defense ETF (XAR) provide exposure to companies that are likely to benefit from increased military spending.

Which Defense ETF is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the three defense ETFs mentioned above, analysts think that XAR has the most room to run. In fact, XAR's price target of $308.11 per share implies 8.3% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Why Mark Zuckerberg is the Key to META's Dividend Future
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) has declared its latest quarterly dividend of $0.525 per share, marking its ninth consecutive cash payout since launching its dividend policy in early 2024. That move — paired with a massive $50 billion share repurchase program — signaled a renewed focus on returning value to shareholders. Despite Meta's stock being down 2.5% so far this year, investors will still be watching closely for their dividend checks. And standing to receive the largest payout of all is Meta's CEO, Mark Zuckerberg.

data.title: Mortgage Rates Drop to Near 4-Year Low as Pending Home Sales Tumble to Record Low
data.description:
Homes just got a bit more affordable. The average 30-year fixed-rate mortgage fell to 6.01%, the lowest level since September 2022, data from Freddie Mac (FMCC) showed on Thursday. The average rate was 6.09% during the previous week and 6.85% a year ago.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Home price growth has also eased, rising by less than 2% on an annual basis over the past 10 months. "With far more homes for sale than people who want to buy them, the buyers who are in the market have the power to negotiate on price, which is keeping price growth in check," said Redfin Senior Economist Asad Khan.

Affordability Gains Fail to Boost Pending Home Sales

Despite incremental improvements in affordability, pending home sales in January dropped by 0.8% to an all-time low going back to 2001, building on a 7.4% decline in December, according to the National Association of Realtors (NAR). NAR Chief Economist Lawrence Yun noted that potential homebuyers could drive up home prices unless more supply becomes available.

In recent months, President Trump has issued several measures in an attempt to improve affordability, including an executive order barring large institutional investors from purchasing single-family homes and calling on Freddie Mac and Fannie Mae (FNMA) to purchase $200 million of mortgage-backed securities.

data.title: DELL, SPOT, UNH: The World's Biggest Hedge Fund Is Bottom Fishing the Market
data.description:
Bridgewater Associates, the world's largest hedge fund with $136 billion of assets under management, bottom-fished the market during the fourth quarter of 2025.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Bridgewater's latest regulatory filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) shows the firm opened new positions in beaten-down stocks such as Dell Technologies (DELL), Spotify (SPOT), and UnitedHealth Group (UNH) during Q4 of last year. Each stock is in the red over the past 12 months.

Bridgewater purchased 156,332 shares of DELL stock, 23,540 shares of SPOT stock, and a small stake of 6,170 shares in UNH stock. The hedge fund also opened new positions in Caterpillar (CAT) through the purchase of 62,157 shares and increased its stake in Eli Lilly (LLY) to 73,292 shares from 10,441 previously.

The Stocks Bridgewater Sold

In addition to opening several new positions, Bridgewater also dumped many stocks from its portfolio in the final months of last year. These included selling all the shares it owned in Affirm Holdings (AFRM), AT&T (T), and insurer Elevance Health (ELV).

Bridgewater Associates also unloaded its position in European weight-loss drug maker Novo Nordisk (NVO) during the most recent quarter, and reduced its stake in biotech concern Moderna (MRNA) to just 15,181 shares from 222,210 previously.

Is LLY Stock a Buy?

Let's look at Eli Lilly, one of the stocks Bridgewater bought more of in Q4 2025. The stock of Eli Lilly has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 20 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 17 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LLY price target of $1,263.78 implies 23.51% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Is HIMS Stock a Buy Ahead of Earnings? Options Traders Brace for 17% Price Swing
data.description:
Hims & Hers (HIMS) will release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results after market close on February 23. Analysts expect earnings to decline but sales to rise modestly in the December quarter

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

HIMS stock faces backlash for selling copycat weight-loss drugs on its pharmacy platform, along with U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) scrutiny and a lawsuit from Wegovy maker Novo Nordisk (NVO). Investors seek further clarity on these issues during the earnings call. Any negative signals could drive shares lower.

Hims & Hers is a telehealth company, offering prescription medications, wellness products, and online consultations. Year-to-date, HIMS shares have plunged more than 51% due to uncertainty around its GLP-1 drug offerings.

Expectations from Hims & Hers

The Street expects Hims & Hers to post adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $0.04, much lower than the prior year's figure of $0.11. Sales are forecast to jump 28.7% year-over-year to $619.20 million. It is worth noting that the company has beaten earnings estimates in six of the past eight quarters.

Moreover, according to TipRanks' Options Traders Tool, options traders expect about a 16.92% move in either direction for HIMS stock in reaction to Q4 FY25 results. This implied move is significantly higher than the stock's average post-earnings move (in absolute terms) of 5.04% over the past four quarters.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: OPEN Earnings: Opendoor Stock Gains after Beating Q4 Estimates
data.description:
Opendoor (OPEN) stock rose in after-hours trading after the online real estate company reported earnings for its fourth quarter of Fiscal 2025. The company reported a loss of $0.07 per share, which was better than the analysts' consensus estimate of a loss of $0.12 per share. In addition, sales decreased 32% year-over-year to $736 million. However, this beat analysts' expectations of $594.04 million.

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Lose Momentum on Record-High Goods Deficit; Trump's Warning to Iran
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed Thursday in negative territory as investors digested a trade balance update from the Commerce Department amid rising tensions between the U.S. and Iran.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Over the course of 2025, the average U.S. import tariff rate rose to 13% from 2.6%. However, that did little to improve the U.S. trade deficit, which edged lower by just 0.2% to $901.5 billion from $904 billion in 2024. In addition, the deficit for December was $70 billion, above the consensus estimate of $55.5 billion and rising from $56.8 billion in November.

Furthermore, the goods trade deficit for 2025 reached an all-time high of $1.24 trillion and widened by 2.1% from 2024. The U.S. carried the largest goods deficit with the European Union at $218.8 billion, with China trailing behind at $202.1 billion and Mexico at $196.9 billion. At the same time, the goods deficit with China was the lowest in 21 years.

Meanwhile, President Trump gave Iran a deadline of around 10 days to either reach a deal with the U.S. to halt its nuclear program or face military action. "Good talks are being had. It's proven to be, over the years, not easy to make a meaningful deal with Iran," Trump said at the first Board of Peace meeting in Washington, D.C. "We have to make a meaningful deal, otherwise bad things happen."

In recent days, the U.S. military has increased its military presence in the Middle East as negotiations continue between the two sides.

Elsewhere, data from the Department of Labor shows that employers remain hesitant to both fire and hire workers. For the week ended February 14, initial jobless claims fell by 23,000 to 206,000, below the consensus estimate of 225,000. Initial jobless claims are at their lowest level in five weeks, while the weekly drop was the largest since November. Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, increased by 17,000 to 1.869 million and were above the estimate of 1.86 million.

"Workers have been finding it more difficult to find new jobs, and soft labor demand may prevent the unemployment rate from declining further in the months ahead," said Bloomberg economist Stuart Paul.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.28% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 0.41%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: "Since I Started Eight Years Ago": Disney Stock (NASDAQ:EADSY) Slips Despite Rumors of ESPN Spinoff
data.description:
Entertainment giant Disney (DIS) has long labored under the notion that, one day, it will spin off ESPN from its operations. This is not a crazy plan. After all, linear television is on something like borrowed time, no matter what company's branding is on it. But one property that is likely safe is ESPN, the sports network giant. And that safety seems to be oddly displeasing to shareholders, based on the fractional slip in Disney shares seen in the closing minutes of Thursday's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

ESPN chairman Jimmy Pitaro has heard the rumors before. In fact, he noted, he has been hearing the idea that Disney might spin off ESPN ever since he started at ESPN, eight years prior. And Pitaro does not look for any kind of spinoff to actually happen.

Pitaro noted, "That's a long way of saying that I believe that ESPN is a big part of the Walt Disney Company's strategy and future. My job is to keep my head down, focus on our strategy, focus on our roadmap, make sure that our people at ESPN are engaged, and that is what we are doing today." Pitaro is not wrong here; after all, Comcast (CMCSA) recently revealed it was dropping $8 billion on live sports content in 2026. The idea that Disney would do much less, therefore, is a long shot.

The Hunt for Ben Solo Goes South

Meanwhile, Disney has not given up on the Star Wars franchise yet…or maybe it has. A new report featured director Steven Soderbergh coming out about The Hunt for Ben Solo, which was supposed to star Adam Driver, but was ultimately shut down by the studio itself. And Soderbergh was not at all happy about how that turned out.

"You know, that was two and a half years of free work for me and Adam and (writer) Rebecca Blunt," Soderbergh said. And the reason Disney shut down the project was oddly simple: "We don't think Ben Solo could be alive." This fundamental blow to the very plotline itself left The Hunt for Ben Solo without much to, you know, hunt.

Is Disney Stock a Buy or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on DIS stock based on 17 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 3.12% loss in its share price over the past year, the average DIS price target of $135.41 per share implies 27.76% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson
